steps.title: Recognize the square
steps.content:
The first three terms form a perfect square.
$$x^4+12ax^2+36a^2=(x^2+6a)^2$$
So the expression becomes a difference of squares.
$$(x^2+6a)^2-25$$
$$(x^2+6a)^2-5^2$$

steps.title: Factor the difference
steps.content:
Use $A^2-B^2=(A-B)(A+B)$ with $A=x^2+6a$ and $B=5$.
$$(x^2+6a-5)(x^2+6a+5)$$
One factor is $x^2+6a+5$. The answer is A.
